assertions.title: No connection leaks under concurrent load

steps.observed: All 50 requests failed with ReadTimeout - server not responding on localhost:8000

steps.action: Verify connection count returns to baseline after requests complete
steps.observed: Connection leak = 0 (but no connections were made due to server unavailability)

evidence.errorDetails: All 50 requests failed with httpx.ReadTimeout after 30 seconds. Server at http://localhost:8000 is not responding.

assertions.issues: Cannot test connection leak behavior because the FastAPI server is not running on localhost:8000. The test attempted to connect but all requests timed out after 30 seconds.

assertions.title: Memory usage stable under load

steps.observed: All 50 requests failed with ReadTimeout - server not responding
Memory growth: 11.62% (49.66 MB post-load vs 43.61 MB pre-load) - but this is test client memory, not server memory

steps.action: Verify memory returns to stable state
steps.observed: Test client memory remained at 49.66 MB after 5 second wait

evidence.errorDetails: Cannot measure server memory usage because server is not running. Measured test client memory only, which grew 11.62% due to httpx connection pool overhead from failed connection attempts.

assertions.issues: Cannot test server memory stability because the FastAPI server is not running on localhost:8000. The memory measurements captured are for the test client process, not the server process.

blockers.description: FastAPI server is not running on localhost:8000. All HTTP requests to /v1/chat/completions failed with ReadTimeout after 30 seconds. Verified with curl that port 8000 is not accepting connections.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified server status with curl and netstat. Port 8000 is not listening. Per flow-validator-guidance.md, validators must not restart the server as it is shared infrastructure. The parent agent or user must start the server before testing can proceed.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both blocked due to server unavailability. The FastAPI server must be running on localhost:8000 before concurrent load testing can be performed. Test infrastructure (httpx, psutil, asyncio) is working correctly - the test script successfully measured baseline metrics and attempted 50 concurrent requests, but all timed out because the server is not responding.
